extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r1 (priority 50): IF (prior_gi_bleed and renal_impairment). rec_drug_b is set true only by r4 (priority 50): IF prior_gi_bleed. Each output has exactly one rule that can set it true, so priorities/tie-breaking are irrelevant and no rule can override these to false; defaults apply only when an output is unset. With prior_gi_bleed=true and renal_impairment=true, r1 fires (rec_drug_a=true) and r4 also fires (rec_drug_b=true) simultaneously, making both true and violating the mutual-exclusion property (rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b). The remaining inputs affect only rec_drug_c and are set false. Thus the property is violated.
